title: Calamagrostis glacialis
intro: Calamagrostis glacialis, also known as Mountain Fescue or Glacier Fescue, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is traditionally used to treat various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis glacialis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calamagrostis glacialis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although the plant is not widely cultivated or used in commercial herbal products due to its limited availability and potential toxicity.
taxonomy: Calamagrostis glacialis is classified as a member of the family Poaceae, which includes grasses. This species falls under the genus Calamagrostis, commonly known as fescue grasses. Calamagrostis glacialis is further divided into the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. It is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. The specific classification of Calamagrostis glacialis also includes its species epithet, which is part of its binomial nomenclature.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds and reducing swelling.
